volumes.title: Vol. I: The Bewildered Boy (1964-1969)
volumes.description: Pre-fame mod era, Anthony Newley influence, Space Oddity breakthrough

volumes.title: Vol. II: The Man Who Sold the World (1970-1971)
volumes.description: Heavy guitar era with Mick Ronson, proto-metal and occult themes

volumes.title: Vol. III: Ziggy Stardust & the Spiders (1971-1973)
volumes.description: Hunky Dory's genius through Ziggy's rise to Aladdin Sane's madness

volumes.title: Vol. IV: The Diamond Duke (1974-1976)
volumes.description: Diamond Dogs' dystopia, Young Americans' plastic soul, Station to Station's Thin White Duke

volumes.title: Vol. V: The Berlin Trilogy (1977-1979)
volumes.description: Low, Heroes, Lodger — Eno collaborations, ambient experiments, the art-rock pinnacle

volumes.title: Vol. VI: The Pop Star (1980-1984)
volumes.description: Scary Monsters' new wave, Let's Dance's commercial triumph

volumes.title: Vol. VII: The Wilderness Years (1987-1991)
volumes.description: Never Let Me Down, Tin Machine — the misunderstood era

volumes.title: Vol. VIII: The Art Chameleon (1993-1999)
volumes.description: Black Tie White Noise, Outside, Earthling, Hours — reinvention after reinvention

volumes.title: Vol. X: The Final Act (2013-2016)
volumes.description: The Next Day, Blackstar — the farewell
